does: This paper introduces PersonaTeaming, a red-teaming framework that incorporates personas into adversarial prompt generation to uncover risks in generative AI systems more effectively. The work develops the PersonaTeaming Workflow, which systematically varies personas during the adversarial prompt generation process to explore a broader range of attack strategies. The authors demonstrate that persona-driven automation achieves higher attack success rates compared to RainbowPlus, a prior state-of-the-art baseline, while preserving diversity across generated adversarial prompts. They also instantiate this approach as PersonaTeaming Playground, extending the framework to enable human-AI collaboration where humans can input their own personas or perspectives to guide red-teaming efforts.
connects: This work extends prior research in automated red-teaming and AI safety evaluation, building on methods like RainbowPlus that use automation to scale the discovery of model vulnerabilities. It responds to emerging insights in AI safety research highlighting that red-teamers' backgrounds and identities significantly influence which risks they uncover, suggesting that perspective diversity improves risk detection. The paper connects to broader human-AI collaboration literature and the growing recognition that purely automated approaches miss important aspects of human-centered evaluation. It addresses limitations in existing automated methods by incorporating the insight that different personas naturally explore different adversarial strategies, creating a more comprehensive attack surface exploration than uniform automation alone.
enables: This work opens pathways for more inclusive and comprehensive red-teaming of generative AI systems by demonstrating that persona variation systematically improves risk coverage. It enables future research into how demographic, cultural, and experiential diversity can be more systematically integrated into AI safety evaluation pipelines. The PersonaTeaming Playground interface particularly enables new forms of human-AI collaboration where diverse human red-teamers can amplify their contributions through automated persona-driven exploration. This approach could inform the development of red-teaming tools that respect and leverage human perspectives while scaling evaluation efforts, potentially leading to better detection of harms relevant to different user populations and more equitable AI safety practices.
